steps.work: We need to find how many 7s are in 63.
steps.explanation: For larger divisors like 7, the best strategy is to think of the related multiplication fact.
Knowing the 7 times table lets you quickly solve division by 7.
Check: 9 × 7 = 63 ✓. Every division can be checked with multiplication.

title: Dividing by 9 Using the Break-Apart Strategy

steps.work: This is beyond the basic times table, so we will break 108 apart.
steps.explanation: When the dividend is larger than what you know from times tables, break it into friendly parts.

steps.instruction: Split 108 into parts that are easy to divide by 9.

steps.explanation: This works because division distributes over addition: (90 + 18) ÷ 9 = 90 ÷ 9 + 18 ÷ 9. Check: 12 × 9 = 108 ✓.
